_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the role of trade unions and employer groups in the workplace and how they advocate for the interests of their members.

_source.w: We are learning about the role of trade unions and employer groups in the workplace and how they advocate for the interests of their members.
y: Trade unions and employer groups are key players in shaping the conditions under which Australians work.
Understanding industrial relations helps us know our rights and navigate the workplace with confidence.
The balance between unions and employer groups reflects broader questions about fairness and power in society.
eg: Investigating the historical role of trade unions in Australia in winning rights like the eight-hour day and fair wages.
Examining the role of employer groups in advocating for business interests in policy and regulation.
Analysing how collective bargaining works and its impact on wages and conditions.
Discussing the decline in union membership and its implications for workers' bargaining power.
Evaluating the role of the Fair Work Commission in resolving workplace disputes.
Examining a contemporary workplace dispute and the roles played by unions and employer groups.
